cards.back: A combination of physical RAM and secondary storage (hard drive or solid state drive) that provides a larger address space to the operating system
A memory management technique that divides the physical RAM into fixed-size blocks called pages and transfers pages between RAM and secondary storage as needed
A reserved area of secondary storage that acts as an extension of physical RAM, used to store pages of memory that are not currently in use
A memory management technique that divides the program into fixed-size segments and assigns each segment a specific memory address
A strategy used to manage the swap space, deciding which pages of memory to replace when the system runs low on physical RAM
